data.title: Implement Printful API integration to create t-shirts from saved designs

data.body:
Implementation complete. 5 commits on server-setup branch (899dd93 → 1ae4917).

What shipped:
- server/internal/printful/ package: client.go (~290 LoC), errors.go (~50), mockup.go (~110), products.go (~120), catalog.go (~70), plus three test files (~520 LoC). Retry policy: 1 retry on 429 (Retry-After up to 10s) and 1 retry on 5xx (500ms backoff); network/timeout surfaces immediately. Token never appears in logs (test guard included).
- server/internal/httpserver/printful_handlers.go (~430 LoC) + tests (~410 LoC). All three routes return 503 with file_id+file_url when Printful is nil; partial failures yield 502 with partial:{mockup_ok, sync_product_ok, ...}.
- main.go reads PRINTFUL_TOKEN/PRINTFUL_STORE_ID/PUBLIC_BASE_URL; missing token logs warn and passes nil; missing PUBLIC_BASE_URL logs separate warn (host fallback works only behind tunnel).
- index.html + script.js: button renamed "Buy Shirt" → "Create T-Shirt"; createTShirt() POSTs /api/printful/products and polls mockup every 1.5s up to 5min; static copies refreshed via copy-static.sh.
- README updated per plan §9: env vars, three endpoints curl'd, architecture bullets on print-file-by-URL, idempotency, parallel fanout; "what's deferred" pruned.

Smoke tests passed:
- 503 path: server boots without PRINTFUL_TOKEN, POST /api/printful/products returns 503 with file_id/file_url; PNG actually saved on disk under DATA_DIR.
- Fake-token path: PRINTFUL_TOKEN=fake-token PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://example.com → 502 with partial body, mockup_ok=false, sync_product_ok=false, both errors "printful 401: The access token provided is invalid." (Printful upstream rejected the fake token, exactly as designed.)

Plan deviations: none material. The 25s fanout timeout (PrintfulFanoutTimeout) was added as a guardrail not specified in the plan but consistent with the spirit of partial-success handling. errgroup goroutines deliberately return nil rather than the error so the sibling continues — partial reporting needs both halves to complete.

needs_human blockers (carry from plan §13):
1. Real PRINTFUL_TOKEN — required to do any end-to-end test against api.printful.com.
2. DefaultVariants[].VariantID values — currently 0 placeholders in catalog.go; will surface as Printful 422 until filled.
3. Public tunnel (ngrok/cloudflared) for PUBLIC_BASE_URL during local smoke testing.
4. Confirm retail_price (placeholder $25.00) and variant-set-in-external_id semantics for V2.

Reviewed cold. acceptance criteria met.

Verified all 10 acceptance criteria pass. `go test ./...` green; `go build` clean; both smoke tests reproduced exactly the documented shapes:

- 503 path: returns {error:'printful_unconfigured', file_id, file_url, message}; PNG written to disk at {file_id}.png — render path runs even without token.
- 502 partial path with fake token: {error:'printful_partial', file_id, file_url, external_id, partial:{mockup_ok:false, sync_product_ok:false, mockup_error, sync_product_error}} — both upstream calls reached Printful and 401'd as expected.

Spot checks all clean: bearer token never logged (TestDoTokenNotInLog); task_id regex ^[0-9]{1,20}$ confirmed (TestMockupStatusValidatesTaskID); singleflight keyed on external_id; errgroup with deliberate return-nil to keep sibling running for partial reporting; 401-upstream → 502-inbound (TestMockupStatusUpstreamErrorTo502); GET-then-POST idempotency hits exactly the asserted call counts (TestCreateTShirtIdempotencyReusesExisting: 2 GETs, 1 POST, 2 mockup POSTs).

PrintfulFanoutTimeout=25s deviation is reasonable: inbound WriteTimeout 60s minus 25s leaves 35s slack; per-call client timeout is 30s so a single hung call still fits. Comment explains intent.

Minor non-blockers (do not require rework): (1) errgroup.WithContext is technically over-spec since both goroutines return nil — plain goroutines+WaitGroup would suffice — but the choice is fine and gctx propagation is clean. (2) The CatalogVariantMockups JSON tag now has omitempty in mockup.go — fine. No security or correctness issues found.
